notes: Many published protocols use DMEM — EMEM is ATCC-authoritative. Subcultivation: add 6-8mL medium aspirate by pipetting.
Subculture when cell concentration is 6-7x10^4 cells/cm2. Cells do not adhere at room temperature — re-attach over several days at 37C.
NOTE: HEK293T uses DMEM not EMEM — differs from HEK293 parent. Complete medium stable 1 month only. Seeding 5x10^4 to 1x10^5 viable cells/cm2.
Confirmed from Vero.STAT1 KO derivative (CCL-81-VHG) — same base medium as parental CCL-81. Some viral production protocols use DMEM+5% FBS.
source_citation: ATCC CCL-81-VHG product sheet confirming EMEM for parental line (verified 2026-06-24)

notes: F-12K confirmed via CCL-185EMT derivative. Common error to assume DMEM for this line.
source_citation: ATCC CCL-185 and CCL-185EMT product sheets (verified 2026-06-24)

notes: NOTE: recombinant human insulin required per ATCC — Thermo cat#12585-014 specified. This is NOT animal-derived. Slow-growing; loosely attached.
source_citation: ATCC HTB-22 and HTB-22dCas9-KRAB product sheets (verified 2026-06-24)

notes: Suspension line — no trypsin. Maintain 1x10^5 to 5x10^6 cells/mL. Renew medium every 2-3 days.
source_citation: ATCC TIB-152; confirmed via Jurkat derivatives TIB-153 and CRL-2676 (verified 2026-06-24)

notes: Per ATCC Animal Cell Culture Guide: calf serum used for contact-inhibition studies with NIH/3T3 CRL-1658. Note: calf serum not FBS — lower growth-promoting properties preserve contact inhibition. Subculture below 75% confluency.
source_citation: ATCC Animal Cell Culture Guide (verified 2026-06-24); Zhou et al. NIH/3T3 application note

notes: Subcultivation ratio 1:2 to 1:4. Reducing serum to 1% promotes myotube fusion per ATCC.
BOTH horse serum (10%) and FBS (5%) required per ATCC CRL-1721. PC-12 Adh variant (CRL-1721.1) uses 15% HS + 2.5% FBS. Horse serum: collected from live donors not slaughtered animals.
source_citation: ATCC CRL-1721 and CRL-1721.1 product sheets (verified 2026-06-24)

notes: 1:1 EMEM:F12 specific to this line — not interchangeable with either medium alone.

*LOW CONFIDENCE: base medium confirmed as DMEM via multiple published protocols citing ATCC HTB-96 but direct ATCC product page did not return full medium spec in search. Verify manually at atcc.org/products/htb-96 before use.
source_citation: Multiple published protocols citing ATCC HTB-96 (verified 2026-06-24)

base_media: mTeSR1 or mTeSR Plus (STEMCELL Technologies cat#05850)
animal_derived_components: Matrigel coating (Corning 354277 — mouse EHS sarcoma origin); xeno-free alternative: Vitronectin XF (STEMCELL cat#07180)
notes: mTeSR1 most widely published feeder-free medium per STEMCELL Technologies. Matrigel standard but Vitronectin XF and Laminin-521 are validated xeno-free alternatives eliminating mouse-derived component.
source_citation: STEMCELL Technologies mTeSR1 protocol and coating guide (verified 2026-06-24)

base_media: RPMI-1640 + B27 supplement (Gibco) for iPSC-CM; M199 + blebbistatin for adult hPCM isolation
animal_derived_components: Matrigel or Laminin coating required for attachment; Collagenase II (bovine) for tissue digestion during isolation
notes: Post-mitotic — do not passage. Animal impact is in coating substrate (Matrigel = mouse EHS) and isolation enzyme (Collagenase II = bovine). Blebbistatin protocol achieves 2.74x improvement in cell viability vs traditional methods. Average yield 3.28x10^6 cells/gram tissue.
source_citation: Zhou et al. Signal Transduction and Targeted Therapy 2022. DOI: 10.1038/s41392-022-01044-5. PMC9325714 (open access). Verified 2026-06-24.
